titleStmt.title: Letter from A.R. Barlas to Matthew Nathan, 18 February 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Alexander Richard Barlas

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from Alexander Richard Barlas (1862-,) the Secretary of the Local Government Board of Ireland to Sir. Matthew Nathan (1862-1939), under-secretary for Ireland. Nathan served as under-secretary for Ireland from 1914 to 1916. He offered his resignation, alongside his chief-secretary, Augustine Birrell (1850-1933), in the aftermath of the Easter Rising of 1916. Barlas writes to forward the under-secretary copies of reports by three of the Local Government Board’s Medical Inspectors on Laboratories in the Universities of Galway, Belfast and Cork, and a Military Barracks in Tipperary. A report from Galway is enclosed. In this report, James Acheson MacCullagh (b. 1855), a medical inspector for the Local Government Board, refers to animal testing in the Physiological Department of University College Galway. MacCullagh reports that the animals were well cared for and comfortable.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/3159

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
No. 8748 : 1916.
Miscelllaneous Local Government Board, DUBLIN, 18th February, 1916. Sir, I am directed by the Local Government Board for
Ireland to transmit, for the information of the Chief
Secretary, the accompanying copies of reports furnished
by the Board's Medical Inspectors, Sir J. Achesson
MacCullagh, Dr. E. F. Stephenson, and Dr. Brendan MacCarthy,
on the Pathological and Physiological Laboratories
attached to the University College of Galway, Belfast
and Cork; and the Outhouse and Laboratory in the
Military Barracks, Tipperary, under the provisions of
Section 10 of the Cruelty to Animals Act, 1876. I am, Sir,
Your obedient Servant, A.R. Barlas Secretary. The Under Secretary, Dublin Castle.
COPY
No. 2330 : 1916. Sir, I beg to report that under Section 10
of the Cruelty to animals Act, 1876, that in
the Physiological Department, University College
Galway, experiments were performed under license.
I have to report that I visited the Department
twice during the year 1915 and on the occasions
of my visits found the animals well cared for and
comfortable. They appeared to be in a healthy
condition. Your obedient Servant, J. Acheson MacCullagh. Medical Inspector. Galway 13th January, 1916.
